cases.threat: Evidence unavailable at the research as-of leaks into a historical answer.
cases.expected: Future evidence remains unavailable.
cases.mitigation: Point-in-time availability filtering.
cases.residual_risk: Incorrect upstream timestamps remain possible.

cases.threat: An amended filing is applied before it became public or the superseded value survives afterward.
cases.expected: Pre- and post-amendment values follow exact availability.
cases.mitigation: Acceptance-time amendment-aware normalization.
cases.residual_risk: Issuer amendment semantics can require human review.

cases.threat: A fact is accepted as both duration and instant or with an incomplete period.
cases.expected: Ambiguous period shape is rejected.
cases.mitigation: Exclusive typed period validation.
cases.residual_risk: Correctly shaped but economically incomparable periods still require comparability policy.

cases.threat: A normalized metric loses its source facts or computation lineage.
cases.expected: Lineage-incomplete metrics are rejected.
cases.mitigation: Required source IDs, transformation, policy, and timestamps.
cases.residual_risk: Lineage proves origin, not semantic correctness.

cases.threat: Incompatible units, currencies, or periods are combined.
cases.expected: The engine emits typed failures and no receipt.
cases.mitigation: Registry-driven metadata validation before dispatch.
cases.residual_risk: A consistently mislabeled source can pass structural validation.

cases.threat: A persisted calculation output is modified after execution.
cases.expected: Hash verification rejects the altered receipt.
cases.mitigation: Canonical receipt hashing and append-only supersession.
cases.residual_risk: Host compromise is outside application integrity guarantees.

cases.threat: A registered financial operation lacks an executable golden path.
cases.expected: Every Tier 0 operation executes with a verifiable receipt.
cases.mitigation: Registry cardinality and complete operation test.
cases.residual_risk: Golden cases do not cover every numeric edge.

cases.threat: A market bar is used before its declared availability.
cases.expected: AvailableAsOf remains false before the boundary.
cases.mitigation: Explicit point-in-time market availability predicate.
cases.residual_risk: Provider publication timestamps are treated as supplied.

cases.threat: Malformed high, low, volume, trades, or symbol enters analysis.
cases.expected: Impossible bar invariants are rejected.
cases.mitigation: Canonical symbol, time, and OHLCV validation.
cases.residual_risk: Plausible but erroneous vendor data requires cross-source controls.

cases.threat: Contradictory values are silently collapsed.
cases.expected: The claim-key conflict remains explicit.
cases.mitigation: Content deduplication plus conflict preservation.
cases.residual_risk: Conflicts without a shared claim key may require semantic review.

cases.threat: A citation is missing, future-dated, or opens an untrusted host.
cases.expected: Resolution fails closed outside the allowlist or as-of boundary.
cases.mitigation: Hash-bound IDs, temporal resolution, and HTTPS host allowlist.
cases.residual_risk: Allowed official pages may later change content.

cases.threat: The context omits required evidence or collapses to one company/document.
cases.expected: Frozen relevant-chunk alignment and complete-evidence rate remain 100%; context diversity and budget hold.
cases.mitigation: Frozen relevance labels, minimum document/company policy, and complete-evidence metric.
cases.residual_risk: Relevant-chunk alignment is not human semantic entailment for arbitrary claims.

cases.threat: A source document instructs the model to override rules, call tools, or reveal secrets.
cases.expected: The statement is redacted, marked missing, loses claim authority, and cannot become source-backed extraction.
cases.mitigation: Deterministic pattern quarantine at prompt, packet, and authorization boundaries.
cases.residual_risk: Obfuscated or novel injections may evade patterns; documents remain untrusted.

cases.threat: Correlation or event timing is presented as causal attribution.
cases.expected: Unsupported causal language is rejected before release.
cases.mitigation: Role authority and causal-language semantic gate.
cases.residual_risk: Subtle causal implication may escape lexical patterns.

cases.threat: The final answer tells the user to buy, sell, or hold a security.
cases.expected: Direct trading instructions are rejected before release.
cases.mitigation: Application-owned responsible-use gate with one bounded repair.
cases.residual_risk: Indirect persuasive phrasing still requires human evaluation.

cases.threat: The answer promises certain returns, profits, or upside.
cases.expected: Guaranteed outcome language is rejected.
cases.mitigation: Deterministic certainty/outcome guard.
cases.residual_risk: Overconfidence without trigger terms can still require semantic review.

cases.threat: A model invents an evidence ID.
cases.expected: Only the affected claim is quarantined and no authority is granted.
cases.mitigation: Closed evidence-ID join after model output.
cases.residual_risk: A wrong claim citing a real ID still depends on reviewer entailment.

cases.threat: Malformed JSON, truncation, timeout, invented references, contradictory review, or a failed specialist corrupts the run.
cases.expected: Retries remain bounded and unsupported output is not released.
cases.mitigation: Unified six-case fake-provider chaos gate.
cases.residual_risk: Hardware and runtime failures have a larger state space.

cases.threat: An unregistered operation or unauthorized role executes a tool.
cases.expected: The call is rejected before execution.
cases.mitigation: Closed capability registry and role authorization.
cases.residual_risk: A vulnerable registered tool remains in scope for tool-specific testing.

cases.threat: Repeated model failures, restart windows, or cancellation leave hanging work or unsafe output.
cases.expected: Retries are bounded, breaker opens and recovers, cancellation returns no answer.
cases.mitigation: Timeout, cancellation, one retry, and bounded circuit breaker.
cases.residual_risk: A full process crash relies on external process supervision.

cases.threat: The parser invents an entity for an unsupported or ambiguous company reference.
cases.expected: The request remains ambiguous with no invented entity.
cases.mitigation: Closed entity resolution and explicit clarification state.
cases.residual_risk: The MVP intentionally supports only the golden company pair.

cases.threat: A follow-up changes company, period, comparison, or as-of without explicit user intent.
cases.expected: Three chained follow-ups preserve governed point-in-time scope and lineage.
cases.mitigation: Typed parent context and lineage reauthorization.
cases.residual_risk: Semantic topic drift still requires broader conversation evaluation.

cases.threat: One case leaks into another or credential-shaped content is retained.
cases.expected: Cases remain identity-bound; unsafe content is rejected; export/delete are explicit.
cases.mitigation: Opt-in SQLite store, projection validation, secret scan, and case-scoped keys.
cases.residual_risk: Disk encryption and multi-user isolation are not claimed.

cases.threat: Prompts, responses, credentials, or chain-of-thought reach traces or the public workspace.
cases.expected: Only closed events and safe projections are exposed.
cases.mitigation: Private atomic traces plus deterministic public projection.
cases.residual_risk: Operating-system administrators can inspect the local process.

cases.threat: The product depends on undeclared environment, secrets, local files, or a dirty build cache.
cases.expected: A fresh binary starts with env -i, serves frontend and APIs, and is ready within five seconds.
cases.mitigation: Temporary build, isolated HOME, fixture mode, and health/readiness checks.
cases.residual_risk: The clean local test is not a container-image pull test.

cases.threat: Concurrent judge interaction destabilizes safe read paths.
cases.expected: Ninety-six concurrent health, config, and golden-case reads complete within five seconds.
cases.mitigation: Race-tested bounded Go HTTP server.
cases.residual_risk: This does not represent concurrent 26B model generation.
